Notice Of Continued Motion To Consolidate For Trial The Cases Against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. And Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. Formerly Known As Kaiser Cement Corporation
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff.
Andrew Sharp of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
CJ Manoli of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 spham28@yahoo.com (Tel: 415-517-5439) (503/CML)

MOTION For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Oct-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Billy L. Smith

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No objections made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to advance the trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The February 13, 2018 trial date is advanced to January 29, 2018 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 7, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is January 11, 2018. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. All other dates, deadlines and notice provisions related to discovery, motions, demands and disclosures remain dictated by relevant provisions of the C.C.P, CRC and Local Rules based on the January 29, 2018 trial date. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by January 22, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on January 25, 2018. Further case management conference on December 6, 2017 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on January 29, 2018 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by January 25, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Disbrow, CSR #7768 (Tel: 415-298-1538) (503/CML)
